title: Cluster Expansion Events
spl:
index=hci sourcetype="hci:cluster_events" earliest=-30d
| search "node added" OR "add node" OR "remove node" OR "expand"
| table _time, cluster, user, action, details
| sort -_time
description: Audits node add/remove, disk group expand, and maintenance mode during cluster scale events.
value: Audits node add/remove, disk group expand, and maintenance mode during cluster scale events.
implementation: Normalize messages from Prism and vCenter. Correlate with change tickets. Alert on unplanned expansion.
visualization: Timeline (expansion events), Table (recent changes), Bar chart (events by cluster).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-nutanix`, vCenter events.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `hci:cluster_events`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize messages from Prism and vCenter. Correlate with change tickets. Alert on unplanned expansion.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="hci:cluster_events" earliest=-30d
| search "node added" OR "add node" OR "remove node" OR "expand"
| table _time, cluster, user, action, details
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cluster Expansion Events** — Audits node add/remove, disk group expand, and maintenance mode during cluster scale events.

Documented **Data sources**: `hci:cluster_events`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-nutanix`, vCenter events. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: hci:cluster_events. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="hci:cluster_events", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Pipeline stage (see **Cluster Expansion Events**): table _time, cluster, user, action, details
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the same scope in the vendor HCI or compute console (UCS, vCenter, Nutanix Prism, and so on).

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline (expansion events), Table (recent changes), Bar chart (events by cluster).
grandmaExplanation: Audits node add/remove, disk group expand, and maintenance mode during cluster scale events — so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
